Yonathan’s reflection for week 2 (cont’d) | I had so much free time and most of my classes are towards the afternoon and practice is at 8pm hence i tend to waste time by scrolling on my phone for the majority of the day that’s why my screen time is so high.

Group Member Slide A - Florish | I was very inconsistent with work this week. Primarily struggling to keep up consistency throughout the semester.

As you can see in my graph, my sleep has been very consistent and I usually get more than 8 hours each night, which helps my recovery. My practice has also been good because I am now fully fit again after my calf strain and feel stronger after spring break. However, my screen time is still high and is averaging around 7 hours a day.

My goal for last week and this week was to reduce my screen time, and I was able to accomplish that. Working on this assignment really made me realize how much time I had been wasting on my phone. Since making this change, I’ve also noticed that my sleep and exercise habits are feeling better. Overall, I’m happy that I achieved my goal, and I want to continue maintaining these improvements moving forward.

Insight #1 - What Days Do I Work the Most? | This is a graph detailing the average time worked on each day of the week. The graph shows that as the week progresses, I work more and more time each day, generally speaking on thursday, were it then massively drops. This could detail that my productivity is impacted by momentum, and I could use this to my advantage to plan myself to be more productive. For example, I could try finishing easy things and focusing on getting time worked in to allow myself to get into a state of focus and work for longer periods of time easier.

Insight #2 - What Days Do I Sleep The Most? | The graph shows that I tend to sleep the most earlier in the week, and after tuesday my sleep drops and stays at around ~5 hours for the rest of the week. The graph slightly correlates with the previous time worked graph. We can deduce the primary reason on wednesday and thursday that I do not sleep is because I spend much of my time working. We cannot make this deduction for the whole week as I sleep the most on Sunday - Tuesday but I also work a lot on those days as well. There are a lot of contributing factors that go into which days I sleep the most and further context is needed in order to draw conclusions.

Insight #3 - Is There A Correlation Between Sleep & Time Worked? | My sleeping pattern this whole semester were very sporadic. This was for a variety of reasons, but overall there was not much consistency. This however, did not strongly affect my time worked. The scatter plot shows a lack of cohesion between the two variables. Although I can take away that many of my lowest work times were days where I slept the most, and vice versa.

Insight #4 - Do I Work More on Days I Don’t Exercise? | The graph displays how my exercise affects my time worked. The graph strongly supports the idea that days I exercise cut into/hamper my productivity in the same day. Going to the gym takes a lot of time, and also expends a good amount of energy which may be affecting my ability to get things done later in the day.
